rationale: Both papers are tightly aligned on socially desirable responding in marketing surveys, and the cited paper contributes a concrete measurement approach (item randomized response with IRT) plus evidence that SDR-scale adjustment may be weak. The citing paper’s note highlights SDR measurement and the limitations of routine control-variable fixes as core themes, so this citation plausibly functions as a methodological or comparative source rather than a generic background mention. Although the match evidence is only DOI-level (no local quote), this is still strong enough to keep for second-stage judgment.
